message:
ft-xbnl0.2.3: inherit ambient Cx in legacy entry points instead of unconditionally forging a fresh request Cx

Small follow-up to the legacy-entry-point Cx routing. The first pass
did `let cx = crate::cx::for_request();` at the top of every
asupersync branch, which always produced a brand-new request-level
Cx unrelated to whatever the caller already had installed. That works
for isolated invocations, but it drops the cancellation chain for
callers who *did* already have an ambient Cx (e.g. the workflow
runner entering through its own request-rooted Cx).

Change the pattern everywhere the legacy entry points acquire a Cx:

    let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

i.e. "if there is an ambient Cx, inherit it; otherwise fall back to
a new request-rooted one". The `unwrap_or_else` branch is only taken
when no ambient Cx is installed on the current task, matching the
semantics of the original `for_request()` call for standalone
callers.

Files changed:

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs
  - `run_with_auth`, `run_with_registry_and_auth`, and
    `run_with_registry_auth_rpc_and_search_config` asupersync
    branches.
  - `shutdown_signal_pending`'s asupersync fallback — same pattern,
    so a nested shutdown-poll helper also inherits the caller's Cx
    instead of rooting a new request.

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs
  - `NativeEventListener::run` asupersync branch.
  - `dispatch_event_with_timeout` asupersync branch.

No behavior change for standalone callers (the fallback is
`crate::cx::for_request` as before). Nested callers now keep their
cancellation chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
